growth_records.medium: Defined anoxic mineral medium (hydrogenotrophic DNRA), chemostat

up.method: calculated theoretical electron demand; supplied 4018 µmol/h in gas (excess)

yields.steady_state: no detectable NO3- (<0.01 mM), 2.0 ± 0.1 mM NH4+ in effluent

growth_records.cite: Heo et al. Hydrogen-dependent dissimilatory nitrate reduction to ammonium enables growth of Campylobacterota isolates. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf092. [PMC12286921]
growth_records.snippet: The A. butzleri hDNRA1 chemostat... reached a steady-state after 526 h... steady-state NO3- consumption and NH4+ production rates were 30.5 ± 0.6 and 28.1 ± 0.4 µmol h-1... Acetate was consumed at a rate of 13.7 ± 0.7 µmol h-1
growth_records.notes: Chemostat dilution rate D=0.025 h-1 (Methods); at steady state mu=D=0.025 h-1. doubling_time = ln2/0.025 = 27.7 h. Rates are reactor-level (600 ml working volume); no biomass concentration reported so cannot convert to mmol/gDW/h. Volumetric: NO3- 30.5/0.6 = 50.8 µmol/L/h. H2 theoretical demand 245 µmol/h vs 4018 µmol/h supplied (H2 in excess, influent=effluent P>.05).

growth_records.medium: Defined anoxic mineral medium (hydrogenotrophic DNRA), batch

up.method: whole-cell Michaelis-Menten; Vmax(app)=3.76 µmol/min/mg biomass, Km(app)=10.8 µM (95% CI 9.0-12.9)
201 ± 1 µmol NO3- completely consumed over batch (2 mM in 100 ml)
670 ± 30 µmol H2 oxidized = 1340 ± 60 µmol e-, 84 ± 4% of theoretical electron demand

sec.units: transient accumulation (higher in hDNRA1 than hDNRA2)

yields.biomass_yield_note: OD600 increase per 1 mM NO3- reduced; ~order of magnitude lower than Pseudomonadota/Bacillota DNRA organisms

growth_records.cite: Heo et al. Hydrogen-dependent dissimilatory nitrate reduction to ammonium enables growth of Campylobacterota isolates. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf092. [PMC12286921]
growth_records.snippet: A. butzleri hDNRA1 completely consumed 201 ± 1 µmol NO3-, of which 181 ± 2 µmol was converted to NH4+... Oxidation of 670 ± 30 µmol H2, yielding 1340 ± 60 µmol e-... Vmax(app) 3.76 µmol min-1 (mg biomass)-1... Km(app) 10.8 µM
growth_records.notes: Batch H2+acetate+NO3-. No mu/doubling reported (diauxic; growth curve Fig 1C only). H2 specific uptake Vmax(app)=3.76 µmol/min/mg biomass x 60 = 225.6 mmol/gDW/h (dry biomass quantified from triplicate 100-ml cultures). NO3-, H2, NH4+ figures are total amounts per bottle over the batch, not rates. Final OD600 0.032±0.002.

carbon_growth.citation: Heo et al. Hydrogen-dependent dissimilatory nitrate reduction to ammonium enables growth of Campylobacterota isolates. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf092. [PMC1228
